summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates arbitrary file read via the {file.*} placeholder. The malicious Caddyfile containing {file./etc/passwd} causes Caddy to read and return the contents of /etc/passwd in the HTTP response, confirming the vulnerability allows reading any file accessible to the Caddy process without validation.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should cause Caddy to read /etc/passwd from the container filesystem and return its contents in the HTTP response, proving that arbitrary file read is possible via {file.*} placeholder injection in configuration.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built Caddy from source, created a malicious Caddyfile with {file./etc/passwd} placeholder, started the container, and the HTTP response to localhost:8080 contained the full contents of /etc/passwd including the 'root:' line, confirming successful arbitrary file read.
verification_method: The run script checks if the HTTP response contains 'root:' which is a signature pattern in /etc/passwd files. This distinguishes a successful file read from an error message or placeholder not being evaluated. The presence of actual /etc/passwd content in the response confirms the vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: Container logs show Caddy started successfully with malicious Caddyfile
HTTP response to localhost:8080 contains full /etc/passwd content
Response includes 'root:' line which is characteristic of /etc/passwd format
No path validation or directory restriction prevented reading /etc/passwd
Placeholder evaluation occurred and file was read into response

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the latest commit, and builds Caddy from source in the Docker image.
reproduction.target_repo_mount_path: N/A - repository cloned inside container during build
